title: Functions & Procedures
description: Create reusable blocks of code with functions to organize and simplify your programs.
fullDescription: Master the art of writing reusable code by creating functions. Learn how to organize complex programs into manageable, reusable blocks.

teacherInstructions.setup: Provide function template cards with placeholders for name, input, and output.
steps: Show repeated code and ask how to avoid duplication (4 min).
Introduce function blocks with one parameterized example (7 min).
Students refactor a repetitive script into two functions (10 min).
Discuss naming conventions and where to reuse each function (6 min).
discussionPrompts: When should we create a new function instead of adding more blocks?
How do good function names help teammates understand code?
tips: Use function naming conventions across the class.
Ask students to test functions in isolation before combining.

teacherGuide: Introduce functions by explaining the concept of reusable code. Show real-life examples of procedures. Demonstrate creating functions with parameters. Have students refactor repetitive code into functions. Emphasize code organization and reusability.

studentExercises.title: Create a Simple Function
studentExercises.description: Create your first function and call it

studentExercises.title: Functions with Parameters
studentExercises.description: Create functions that accept different parameters
